Video + Audio
The Concept

Great Leaders Aren't Born in Corner Offices.
They're Built in Locker Rooms.

Parents invest thousands of hours and dollars in youth sports but often miss the real return on investment: the leadership DNA their kids are building.

Meanwhile, young professionals who played sports rarely connect what they learned on the field to what they need in the boardroom.

Dugout to the Boardroom lives in the intersection. Every week, you walk away with one leadership lesson you can apply today, whether you are coaching a Little League team, running a company, or just trying to be a better parent.

01
Primary Audience

Sports Parents

Ages 30 to 45, driving to practices, sitting in bleachers. You want to understand the deeper lessons your children are learning.

02
Secondary Audience

Young Professionals

Ages 22 to 35, you played sports and now you are navigating early-career leadership. Relatable advice, not corporate jargon.

03
Strategic Audience

Business Leaders

You believe the best teams are built on culture and competitive fire. This show speaks your language.

About the Show

Leadership Lessons from Sports, Business & Life

A weekly podcast translating what you learn on the field into what you need in the boardroom. For parents, professionals, and leaders who believe the best teams are built on culture.

The Problem

Parents invest thousands of hours and dollars in youth sports but often miss the real return on investment: the leadership DNA their kids are building.

Young professionals who played sports rarely connect what they learned on the field to what they need in the boardroom.

The Solution

Dugout to the Boardroom is the only show that takes real sports experiences and reverse-engineers them into parenting advice and career strategy.

Every week, you walk away with one leadership lesson you can apply today, whether you are coaching a Little League team, running a company, or just trying to be a better parent.

The Format

52 episodes per year. Weekly drops every Thursday, 9AM to 11AM EST. Available as both video and audio across YouTube, Spotify, and Apple Podcasts.

Every 3rd or 4th episode will feature a guest. Solo episodes dive deep on one leadership concept. Guest episodes bring in athletes, coaches, and business leaders who have lived in both worlds.

The Connection

Dugout to the Boardroom is proudly produced by Coastal Wealth. The show creates an authentic bridge between the sports community and the families and professionals Coastal Wealth serves.

The target audience of engaged sports parents represents one of the most valuable demographics in financial services: young families who value discipline, commitment, and long-term thinking.

Season One Themes

Q1 / Weeks 1 to 13

Grit, Mindset & Foundation

Starting fast, embracing discomfort, consistency, patience, and why the scoreboard does not show everything.

Q2 / Weeks 14 to 26

Leadership, Culture & Accountability

What makes a great captain, servant leadership, emotional intelligence, handling conflict, and championship habits.

Q3 / Weeks 27 to 39

Strategy, Development & Execution

Risk and timing, adapting to the curveball, building routines, personal brand, and knowing your role.

Q4 / Weeks 40 to 52

Growth, Legacy & Motivation

Mentorship, celebrating unsung heroes, remote team leadership, finishing strong, and planning the next season.

Season One

52 Episodes. 52 Leadership Lessons.

Every week, one concept from the field translated into the boardroom. Solo deep-dives and guest conversations with athletes, coaches, and business leaders.

Your Host

Luis "Wicho" Hernandez

Former minor league ballplayer. Harvard psychologist. Managing Partner. College baseball broadcaster. A career spent building teams in both the clubhouse and the corporate world.

The Athlete

Wicho earned his Bachelor's degree in Business Administration from the University of Miami (1991 to 1994), where he was a four-year letterman as a baseball player. After his college career, he played minor league baseball for the New York Mets in 1994.

The Scholar

He went on to earn a Master's degree in Industrial Psychology from Harvard University in 1996, combining his competitive drive with a deep understanding of human performance and organizational behavior.

The Business Leader

Wicho is currently serving as Managing Partner of Coastal Wealth (formerly MassMutual Miami), headquartered in Coral Gables with additional offices in Miami, Fort Lauderdale, and Ohio. Over one hundred employees throughout the various locations call Coastal Wealth the preferred place of employment.

He is recognized as a leading businessman in the field of financial services, focusing on fulfilling the primary needs of corporations while educating families in the importance of planning for the future.

The Community Leader

Wicho is a Founding Director of Apollo Bank and currently serves on its Board of Directors. He founded Wicho's On The Green, a charity golf tournament in its 11th year that has raised money for organizations including the Autism Society Miami-Dade Chapter, MS South Florida, Children's Cancer Caring Center, and Miami's Lighthouse for the Blind.

In 2013, Baptist Hospital recognized Wicho as the Philanthropic Volunteer of the Year.
